cpSummary: Predominantly parents of kindergarten- and school-age children buying in response to outbreak alerts from daycare or school. A small share are grandparents and teachers; almost none buy for adult self-use. Purchase moments concentrate around active outbreak notifications and the school-year cycle, with daily application at home before school or daycare.

usageScenarios.label: Daily prophylaxis during Kita/school outbreak
usageScenarios.reason: Parents apply the product every morning once daycare or school notifies of lice cases

usageScenarios.label: Weekly preventive shampoo routine
usageScenarios.reason: Used 1x per week as ongoing prevention during peak lice season

usageScenarios.label: Post-treatment maintenance
usageScenarios.reason: Applied after an apothecary treatment to prevent re-infestation

usageScenarios.label: Braid / tied-up hair plus spray
usageScenarios.reason: Combined with tight braids or ponytail to physically deter head-to-head transfer

usageScenarios.label: Whole-family preventive use
usageScenarios.reason: Parent treats themselves and all siblings at once when one case is reported

usageScenarios.label: Carried to Kita/school daily
usageScenarios.reason: Child wears gummies/spray all day as passive protection

csSummary: Highly polarized sentiment. Roughly 39% of reviews are 1-2 star, dominated by one message: the product failed to prevent lice (often alongside damaged deliveries and cheap-smelling perfume). The 42% positive side genuinely credits the products for keeping kids lice-free through Kita outbreaks, and praises the smell and ease of use. The gap between the two camps is almost entirely about whether customers treat it as a guarantee versus a risk-reducer.

negativeTopics.label: No efficacy — child still got lice
negativeTopics.reason: The single largest complaint: parents applied the product as directed and their child still brought lice home from Kita or school
bullets: Cuts across all 5 ASINs (gummies, sprays, shampoos)
Often reported alongside an apothecary rescue treatment
Frequently framed as 'money out the window'
Damages trust in the entire prevention category
quotes: "After extensive testing, I cannot recommend these anti-lice gummies. My daughter got lice despite wearing them."
"My children still got lice despite wearing them constantly. Not recommended."
"I applied it exactly as directed to my child during the school outbreak. She still came home with lice."
"Nothing I liked. Protection against lice — it wasn't."

negativeTopics.label: Build quality / elastic breaks / sprayer defective
negativeTopics.reason: Hair gummies tear or lose elasticity within days; spray bottles arrive with broken nozzles or leaking
bullets: Gummies lose stretch after 3 wears
Multiple reviewers received leaking/open bottles
Sprayer mechanism breaks after first use
Customers report no refund or return possible
quotes: "After about 3 wears the gummy falls apart and the scent is completely gone."
"They tear too quickly because they are not very stretchy."
"The spray nozzle doesn't work at all — not a single drop came out."
"Product arrived leaked, damaged, and no right to exchange it."

negativeTopics.label: Smell too strong / chemical / unpleasant
negativeTopics.reason: Essential-oil or strawberry fragrance is so intense that parents, children or teachers find it off-putting
bullets: Eucalyptus/tea tree scent gives parents headaches
Strawberry version described as cheap artificial candy smell
Children refuse to wear it to Kita out of fear of being stigmatized
Scent lingers on hands and hair all day
quotes: "The gummies smell so strongly that I got a headache. I didn't want my daughter to use them at Kita for fear of stigma."
"It has this cheap fruity smell and it is very, very strong. Even after washing my hands several times the smell stayed for hours."
"Unbearable, nauseating artificial smell. I would never have bought it had I known."

negativeTopics.label: Greasy / sticky residue on hair
negativeTopics.reason: Sprays and conditioners leave hair looking oily, stringy or unwashed
bullets: Reported for both spray ASINs
Makes hair look unwashed after application
Some parents stop using it for cosmetic reasons
Perceived as 'greasy shield' rather than preventive barrier
quotes: "It is so oily that hair looks stringy and unwashed afterwards."
"It greases the hair — exactly what I didn't want."
"I find the hair looks a bit greasy/sticky after spraying."

negativeTopics.label: Damaged / leaking delivery, no returns
negativeTopics.reason: Multiple bottles arrived open, leaking, or without caps — and Amazon refused returns on the category
bullets: Especially common for the spray ASIN
No return possible (hygiene category)
Triggers immediate 1-star even before trying the product
Packaging described as inadequate
quotes: "Product leaked out, delivered damaged, and no right to exchange it. Thanks for the money for damaged goods!"
"It arrived like this — without a box, no seal, no cap. Looks used or second-hand."
"Bottle arrived open, loose cap, not in original packaging."

negativeTopics.label: Poor value / too expensive for what you get
negativeTopics.reason: Runs out fast, only 4 applications per bottle, not cost-effective for long-hair or multi-child families
bullets: 100ml spray runs out in 1-2 weeks with long hair
Gummies at €12 seen as a flop for 1 month of use
Multi-child families burn through a bottle in days
Apothecary alternatives seen as better value
quotes: "For €12 this is a real flop, I won't reorder."
"Bottle runs out very fast with long hair. For the price I would have wished for a more generous solution."
"Bad price-performance ratio. It's only good for 2 weeks."

negativeTopics.label: Skin irritation / scalp reaction
negativeTopics.reason: Minority but serious complaint: eczema, itchy scalp, dried-out hair
bullets: Eczema on scalp reported
Dries out hair — breakage after weeks of use
Intolerance in sensitive children
Raises concern about essential-oil concentration
quotes: "The product is bad because it causes eczema on the scalp. I don't recommend it."
"My daughter's long healthy hair became very brittle after weeks of use."
"Scent is ok, but unfortunately they are incompatible for us."

negativeTopics.label: Labeling / description unclear
negativeTopics.reason: Shampoo is actually an after-treatment care product, not a lice killer — customers feel misled
bullets: Shampoo ASIN is a post-treatment cleanser, not insecticidal
Listing on German Amazon in Spanish only
Customers expect active lice killing but get prevention only
Creates strong disappointment on first use
quotes: "Not an anti-lice shampoo. It's only for care AFTER a lice treatment. The shampoo itself can do nothing against lice!!!"
"Description only in Spanish. No idea what this is."
"It's a lice repellent, it doesn't kill them when you actually have lice."

negativeInsights.finding: Customers expect 100% lice prevention, but essential-oil-based products are only a deterrent that reduces — not eliminates — transmission risk.
negativeInsights.implication: Reframe positioning as 'risk reduction during outbreaks' instead of 'prevention guarantee'. Every 1-star 'my child still got lice' review is an expectation-setting failure, not a product failure.

negativeInsights.finding: Negative build-quality feedback (torn gummies, broken sprayers, leaking bottles) is almost entirely a supply-chain and QC issue, not a formula issue — yet it's bleeding into the overall star rating.
negativeInsights.implication: A sealed blister pack for gummies and a reinforced sprayer cap would reclaim ~19% of negative reviews without touching the formula.

negativeInsights.finding: Intense eucalyptus/tea-tree smell is cited both as proof of efficacy AND as a reason kids refuse to wear it (social stigma at Kita).
negativeInsights.implication: A milder-scent SKU positioned for 'discreet school wear' would unlock an under-served segment without alienating the 'strong smell = strong protection' believers.

negativeInsights.finding: German Amazon listings appear with Spanish-only descriptions and mis-categorized 'lice shampoo' that is actually a post-treatment cleanser — parents feel actively misled.
negativeInsights.implication: Rewrite PDPs in clean German with an explicit 'this is PREVENTION, not a lice killer' disclaimer at the top of bullet points. Will cut the 'Unnötig / Nicht-Wirkung' complaints dramatically.

negativeInsights.finding: Over 30% of negative reviews mention the apothecary as the fallback — suggesting customers trust pharmacy brands more than Amazon-only brands for efficacy.
negativeInsights.implication: Lead with clinical-style evidence (tested at X, pediatrician-approved, German pharmacy-grade) to close the trust gap with apothecary competitors.

negativeInsights.finding: Many 3-star reviewers are 'hopeful but unconvinced' — they haven't seen lice yet but can't tell if the product helped or they just got lucky.
negativeInsights.implication: Add a simple weekly 'lice check' comb routine card in-pack, so customers have a proof ritual that links the product to a visible outcome.

positiveTopics.label: Worked — no lice during Kita/school outbreak
positiveTopics.reason: The single biggest positive: customers credit the product with keeping kids lice-free through an active outbreak
bullets: Cited across all 5 ASINs
Usually paired with 'the whole class had lice'
Often triggers a repeat purchase
Strong emotional relief language ('thank goodness')
quotes: "We have used them for a year consistently in Kindergarten whenever there's a lice warning, and so far we have not had lice once! Just reordered."
"Since we use the defense spray, we've had no problems with head lice anymore, even though the daycare and school have had repeated cases."
"We survived the entire Kita lice season completely lice-free! And the hair shines beautifully."
"Even the Kita teachers use it! We were also spared."

positiveTopics.label: Pleasant fragrance — kids actually like wearing it
positiveTopics.reason: Strawberry / floral / mild herbal scents are a win; children don't resist application
bullets: Strawberry variant most loved by kids
Doesn't smell 'medical' like apothecary alternatives
Kids ask for it themselves
Lingers pleasantly, not overpowering
quotes: "Smells pleasantly of strawberry candy and works well against lice so far."
"Kids love the strawberry smell. One point off because it dries hair slightly."
"We personally love this extreme strawberry scent that lasts long — so far keeping lice away from us."

positiveTopics.label: Easy and fast daily application
positiveTopics.reason: Spray-and-go routine is simple enough to integrate into pre-Kita morning rush
bullets: No discussions with reluctant kids
Fits into pre-school routine
Non-greasy when used correctly
Pump mechanism smooth
quotes: "Application is simple and fast in daily life. It spreads well without greasing or smelling unpleasant."
"Easy handling, no mess at all. Used it a few times on my kid's hair and it didn't feel sticky or heavy."
"Very pleasant to use — like a conditioner. Leaves shiny, easy-to-comb hair."

positiveTopics.label: Peace of mind / parental confidence
positiveTopics.reason: Even unsure reviewers value the emotional reassurance during outbreak panic
bullets: Parents report feeling in control
Reduces the Kita-notification panic
'Good for my conscience' framing
Preferred over doing nothing
quotes: "Effect not proven but good for the conscience :) I bought them to offer my child some level of protection."
"Worth trying if you need some peace of mind."
"The peace of mind that it will be harder for her to catch lice is worth it."

positiveTopics.label: Doubles as a regular styling product
positiveTopics.reason: The gummies work as normal hair ties; the spray detangles; shampoos smell nice — bonus utility
bullets: Gummies used for regular ponytails
Spray detangles like a conditioner
Shampoo doesn't dry hair
Kids see it as 'normal' not 'medicine'
quotes: "They look good, smell fine and have good spring. I would buy them again even without the lice feature."
"It detangles hair and probably works as a repellent since it contains tea tree."
"Foams well and has a pleasant smell. My children also like it."

positiveTopics.label: Cheaper than apothecary alternatives
positiveTopics.reason: For recurring prevention, Amazon-priced bottles are far cheaper than pharmacy products
bullets: One bottle lasts a whole family a Kita season
Cheaper than pharmacy
Long shelf-life in the original packaging
Good bulk value (20-pack gummies)
quotes: "Super effect, simple to apply, we'll buy again for a new outbreak. Cheaper than at the pharmacy."
"The 20-pack is very well-meaning. In theory it lasts almost a year."
"Price is OK given how long it lasts and especially when you don't have any more trouble!"

positiveInsights.finding: The most powerful positive language is emotional — 'we survived the lice season', 'such relief', 'thank goodness'. Parents are selling PEACE OF MIND, not active ingredients.
positiveInsights.implication: Hero image and first bullet should lead with the emotional outcome ('survive the next Kita lice alert lice-free') rather than ingredient lists.

positiveInsights.finding: Kids choosing to wear the product themselves is a recurring positive signal — strawberry variant specifically drives zero-friction application.
positiveInsights.implication: Create a 'kid-approved scents' sub-line (strawberry, apple) and promote it with kid-POV content showing the child reaching for it.

positiveInsights.finding: Repeat purchasers describe a weekly ritual (Sunday + Wednesday shampoo, daily spray before Kita) — they anchor the product into an existing routine.
positiveInsights.implication: Ship with a printed 'weekly prevention calendar' magnet for the fridge. Turns a one-off buy into a habit and locks in repeat sales.

positiveInsights.finding: Teachers and Kita staff recommending the product is a high-signal social proof that currently lives only in reviews.
positiveInsights.implication: Pursue a Kita/Erzieherinnen endorsement program and surface those testimonials in A+ content ('used by Kita staff across Germany').

positiveInsights.finding: Positive reviewers always combine the product with another behavior (tight braids, weekly comb checks, whole-family treatment). None credit the product alone.
positiveInsights.implication: Package the product as a 'prevention protocol' bundle: spray + comb + braid guide. Frames it as a system, which matches how the happy customers actually use it.

positiveInsights.finding: Customers who explicitly compare to apothecary products praise the lower cost AND the milder smell over pharmacy brands (Goldgeist etc.).
positiveInsights.implication: Run direct comparison A+ content: 'pharmacy-grade protection at half the price, without the chemical smell.'

buyersMotivation.label: Avoid lice after Kita/school outbreak alert
buyersMotivation.reason: Primary trigger — Kita notification sends parents shopping

buyersMotivation.label: Break the reinfestation cycle
buyersMotivation.reason: Families already treated once want to stop lice coming back from school

buyersMotivation.label: Protect long-haired daughters
buyersMotivation.reason: Long hair seen as higher-risk; parents invest specifically for girls with long hair

buyersMotivation.label: Pediatric-friendly alternative to apothecary chemicals
buyersMotivation.reason: Parents want essential-oil-based option over harsh Goldgeist-type products

buyersMotivation.label: Peace of mind during peak season
buyersMotivation.reason: Emotional reassurance during Kita/school outbreaks, even without proof of effect

buyersMotivation.label: Multi-child household protection
buyersMotivation.reason: Need one product to cover all siblings at once

customerExpectations.label: Guaranteed 100% lice prevention
customerExpectations.reason: Customers expect zero lice if the product is used as directed — and rate 1-star when they get lice

customerExpectations.label: Actively kills live lice
customerExpectations.reason: Customers assume 'lice shampoo' or 'lice spray' will eliminate an active infestation, not just prevent one

customerExpectations.label: Durable build quality (gummies, sprayer, packaging)
customerExpectations.reason: Product should survive real-world use for the full stated period — not tear after 3 wears or arrive broken

customerExpectations.label: Mild, non-stigmatizing scent
customerExpectations.reason: Child should not smell medicinal enough to be singled out at Kita

customerExpectations.label: Cosmetic compatibility (non-greasy hair)
customerExpectations.reason: Hair should still look clean after application — no oily residue

customerExpectations.label: Clear German labeling and honest category
customerExpectations.reason: Listing should explicitly say prevention vs killing, in German, with realistic usage counts

reviews.t: After extensive testing, I unfortunately cannot recommend these anti-lice gummies. My daughter got lice despite wearing them. Very disappointed and not worth the money.
After about 3 wears the gummy falls apart, plus the smell is completely gone.
My children got lice despite wearing them constantly. Not recommended.
Lousy quality. They tear too quickly because they are not very stretchy (for tying ponytails).
Short durability. The effect is debatable, but the durability of the band itself is poor.
Zero effect! It was a lost cause.
Warning! The product is bad because it causes eczema on the scalp. I do not recommend it.
Doesn't help at all! I let myself be swayed by the consistently positive reviews. They definitely do NOT keep lice away!!
Doesn't help at all. I should have listened to the negative reviews — these things help zero!!! Maybe someone has a different tip.
Expensive hair ties with no effect. Unfortunately disappointed. Despite the gummies and an additional Protect&Go spray from the pharmacy, my child came home with uninvited 'friends' again.
Breaks fast. Not good.
Doesn't work. Nothing I liked. Protection against lice — it wasn't.
Well... The hair gummies smell strong and we couldn't detect any effect.

Once top, once flop. We ordered them because of lice at school. The first packs smelled of geranium oil — not unpleasant, so you could imagine they work. This time unfortunately not a single gummy smells of the essential oils, if anything they just smell of rubber. A real flop for €12, won't reorder.
Unfortunately not quality.

Fine as hair ties, but no for lice. I bought this product thinking you wouldn't get lice — far from it. The smell doesn't even last one day. Shame, given the overwhelming reviews I really thought it would deliver what it promises.
Smell good, rubber band very bad. The bands smell pleasantly of herbs. As for durability, I can't write anything good. Even new they're not really firm, and after a few days no longer usable at all.
I'm disappointed. 2 out of 5 packs have no scent at all, colors are all doubled — I thought they came in different colors.
Gummies. They did not produce any result.
Nice hair gummies. Unfortunately they are incompatible.
Hair gummies aren't good. Not satisfied — they broke way too fast because they are too thin.
Not recommended. They smell nice of tea tree, but whether they really keep lice away is questionable. Unfortunately the hair gummies also stretch (go slack) very quickly.
Hair gummy. Doesn't help my daughter — doesn't work, and the smell doesn't help.
Lice rubber bands. Smell very good but I can't say whether they protect — still got lice.
You can try them. Good quantity. Could be thicker — unsuitable for thick hair. Strong scent and only 15 days of effectiveness per the packaging.
More for the parents' conscience. Lice shampoo and comb are the gold standard.
No idea. Smells pleasant but I don't know if they bring the desired solution. You can believe in them or not.

Effect not provable, very strong eucalyptus smell, also good as regular hair ties. Elastic, good hold. The difference is just the rather strong eucalyptus smell when opening the pack. No lice have struck yet, but that could be luck.
Excitement is different. Tears very quickly, whether it works is questionable.
Hair gummies against lice. Can't report anything negative yet — my granddaughter wears them since lice are constantly around at Kita. The only thing I can say is they smell strongly of citronella.
Doesn't do anything. Couldn't tell any success except that it smells a bit.
Very strong smell. The gummies smell so strongly that I got a headache. I also didn't want my daughter using them at Kita out of fear of being stigmatized due to the strong smell.
Thumbs up emojis only. Neutral rating.
Can't say yet whether the gummies help. They smell pleasant to me and I hope they will help.

Effect not proven but good for the conscience. I bought the anti-lice hair gummies to offer my child a certain protection. Comes in various colors, pleasant scent — not overpowering. Daughter tolerates them without problem. You have to be aware these gummies can never be 100% protection.
No more critters so far. After my daughter repeatedly brought lice home from school, I got these gummies. They are very soft and stretch out quickly. But since then no new incident. That's great.
Four stars. Nice but expensive.
Confusing. I didn't understand whether they have an effect or not.

Good hair gummies. We ordered them despite ongoing treatment. They don't smell too strong and hold well. Whether they now drove the lice away or worked in combination with the other treatment, I can't say.
All good.
Hair gummy. Thanks a lot.
I'm satisfied. The gummies hold well, not too thick, rarely disturb sleep, don't stretch out after a week of wear, seem to work (preventively). Downside: can't choose colors.

Hair gummies. Amusingly, a nit/lice comb was also included as prevention. Scent is quite intense but not bad. Unfortunately they stretch out quite quickly. Still waiting to see if they really prevent lice.
Good quality. Look good, don't smell bad, have good tension. Not as hard as some other hair gummies. I'd buy them again even without the lice-prevention aspect.
OK. Gummies generally good. Scent quite strong. We'll see if they're effective.
Hair gummies. Hope it works.

No lice. Bought them for my daughter in Kita when there was a lice infestation. Whether it was really the hair gummies or we just got lucky, I don't know — but the main thing was 'No lice!' They smell pleasant and hold well even on very straight, soft hair.
We've never had lice. We've used them consistently in Kindergarten for a year whenever there's a lice warning (plus tight lice-safe braids) — touch wood, we haven't had lice once! Just reordered.

Strong scent but seems to work. We used the anti-lice hair gummies after lice were found at school. The product worked well and afterwards the head was lice-free. The smell is strong at first but not unpleasant.
Good.
OK. All OK.
Top.
Good emoji.
Good.
Hair gummies. Super great. Can recommend them.
Super. Delivers what it promises.
Super quality and product.
They do what they should. Since we have the rubber bands we have no more problems. Also reusable if you put them back in the packaging.

It smells so strong! It has this cheap fruity smell and it is very strong. I washed my hands several times but the smell stayed for hours. My poor child had it on his head. I only used it once — can't really comment on effectiveness. The awful smell might keep infected children away.
Hands off. Product leaked, delivered damaged, no right to exchange. Thanks for the money for damaged goods! Better keep your hands off this.
What is this, actually? Description only in Spanish. No idea what it is, so I won't put it on myself or my children. Return wasn't possible, straight to the trash.
Ineffective. Lots of money, no effect, no return possible. Fail.
Nauseating smell. It has an unbearable nauseating artificial smell. I would have never bought it had I known.
Damaged.
Cloying smell. Can't stand the smell, and it's very intense. Had to apply it on the terrace and ended up throwing it out.
No cap — what a disgrace. Came without a cap. Unbelievable that it arrives with no cap, no specific packaging or box — just loose without a cap.
Disastrous. Terrible — switched to this product and my daughter caught lice. Never had them before and after switching she got them immediately. Won't buy it again.
Scam. Arrived without box, no seal, no cap. Looks used or second-hand. They won't let me return it. I tried it and it leaves hair matted and doesn't detangle.
Greases hair. It greases the hair — exactly what I didn't want. Smells very good but leaves hair dirty.
Fruity. Didn't work for us.
Doesn't detangle and dries out hair. I don't know if it wards off lice, but it dries hair and creates knots instead of detangling them.

Doesn't work as a repellent. Bought as prevention and it really didn't work — I had to apply an active treatment in the end. At least it smells good and helps detangle, but its main function is unfulfilled.
Leaves residue. Detangles but leaves a residue that makes hair look greasy. Smells very strongly of strawberry, much more than alternatives. Not buying again.
Intense smell.

Unpleasant smell and doesn't detangle. The smell is unpleasant, very strong, lasts all day. Doesn't detangle; you have to apply so much product that the smell gets even stronger and dirties the hair. You use it after washing and you have to wash again.
Didn't work. It didn't work as a lice repellent and leaves a strong tea-tree smell.

Haven't used even a third. Smells really bad — very strong chemical smell. Leaves hair sticky as if you had sprayed dirty oil. Don't know if it's effective at preventing lice because we've barely used it. Ended up buying another brand.
Stinks! Doesn't work very well. What's for sure is that no one comes near you with the stench.
Smells awful. Smells of a thousand devils — no apple scent detectable, smells like medicine.
Comes without cap. The product came without a cap.
Smells good, success? So far no success, but it smells great and the kids like to use it.
Strong smell, effect present. Smells very strong, but so far it's doing what it promises.
Perplexed. Good smell but I wonder if it's really effective.
Nothing special. Leaves hair greasy.
Strong smell. My child didn't like it — the smell is 'too sweet'. Maybe I should have tried the apple scent.
Good... Don't know how to define it. Helps with styling and leaves a good aroma. Don't know if the lice claim is true since fortunately nobody near us had them.
Says apple scent but only smells intensely of tea tree.
Smells very good. Smells nice but doesn't repel lice much and doesn't detangle. I used it every day and she still got lice.
Bad finish. Using it on a girl. Does the job — problem is how it leaves the hair, as if greasy/gelled. Will finish this bottle but won't buy again.
Didn't work for me. She got them anyway.
Intense smell. Smell isn't very pleasant. Don't feel a big difference when combing.
So-so. Good but the smell isn't my thing.
Best prevention against lice. The spray is definitely effective and a good preventive. Also serves as a conditioner. Kids love the strawberry smell. One point off because it dries hair slightly.
Smells good. Smells good and suitable for the purpose, just slightly sticky.

Does its job well. Detangles and probably works as a lice repellent because it has tea tree. I've read reviews saying it smells bad — it doesn't. A strong strawberry aroma fades into the tea tree scent.
Good product. Works very well but sticky texture.
Helps contain spreading of nits. No new lice, nits moving outward. Product seems effective after 2 weeks of use.
Pleasant smell. Has a very pleasant smell and works well for lice.
Effective. I like it because my daughter hasn't caught lice since we started using it, but it has a very strong smell. I prefer the strawberry one.
Smell ok. Tea tree smell.
The smell. It's a detangler that also helps avoid parasites and my girl loves the strawberry scent. We use it as a complement to shampoo and between the two she hasn't caught anything.
Very good. Good product, recommended.
Smells intensely! We love it. Personally we love this extreme strawberry scent that lasts — keeps lice away from us so far.
Good protection. Pleasant scent like strawberry candy and works well against lice. They were going around in Kita and we stayed free of them. Clear recommendation, will reorder.
Great product, works.
Very good. Scent very pleasant. Despite an outbreak at school, nothing happened to my kids. Sprayed every morning before school.
Super. Perfect.
Thumbs up.
Lice spray. My daughter had no lice, smells pleasant, easy to use.
MEGA. Awesome.
No lice. Even the Kita teachers use it! We were also spared. Product arrived neatly packaged. No problems.
No lice so far. They said there were lice in the class so we bought this spray. We stayed safe. Pleasant scent and doesn't make hair greasy.
Great product. Delivers what it promises. My daughter got rid of her lice very quickly.
Not an anti-lice shampoo. Only for care AFTER a lice treatment. The shampoo itself can do nothing against lice!!
No effect. Unfortunately it didn't meet expectations.
Unnecessary. This shampoo is only for after-treatment of lice infestation, not an anti-lice shampoo. However, it works strongly drying.

If children bring something from school, use something else. Whoever wants this for active lice treatment — from our experience, it was useless. I now use it as a regular shampoo, smells pleasant. For active treatment use Goldgeist.
Not for killing the lice. Useless. Not for killing, only to prevent. But I'm not sure if it prevents anything.
Bottle arrived open. Package arrived open, loose cap, not in original packaging. Worst thing — not eligible for return despite Prime.
Repellent, not an insecticide. It's a lice repellent. Doesn't work when you have actual lice. Doesn't kill them.
Ineffective. Bought to change the active ingredient for recurring lice — did not show effectiveness despite scrupulous use.
Doesn't work. I don't recommend this. Tried it on my girl and it had no effect — didn't remove dead insects or nits. Not worth the price. Better alternatives out there.
Awful. Don't buy it, doesn't work at all. Bought the old lice shampoos again. This does nothing — wasted money.
Useless. Doesn't function.
Useless. Serves no purpose.
Not recommended. Fast delivery but didn't help. Can't understand the positive ratings.
One wash doesn't give results. Good smell but didn't work well on first attempt. Let's see if it can actually remove the lice.
So-so. Use as a complement only, not great.
Tested for 5 years. Sulfates and sodium benzoates aren't good for hair.
Shampoo. Courier left the box on stairs. Product arrived without comb, without box, without the instruction leaflet!
It worked.
Kindergarten and first grade. I don't know if it works — I use it twice a week on each child. No lice recently, but we did have lice while already using this shampoo once a week. Smells very good.
Useless. Didn't work.
Tried. Tried it but unsatisfactory.
Not on my daughter. Still comes home with lice despite the care.
Not a live shampoo. It's a shampoo for after-treatment of lice. Won't remove lice.
Good but nothing special. Nice very mild scent but doesn't stay on hair. Mix of herbs. Doesn't tip the scale toward certainty that lice won't come back. Not very preventive but pleasant to use.
Fine.
Doesn't work for repeat cases. Only works the first time.

Three stars (one for conditioning, one for courage, one for smell) but doesn't solve anything. Applied as instructed like a sacred rite. But the lice laughed. The scent is great, aromatic, purifying. Too delicate maybe — as if asking parasites to leave gently. Lice don't have manners.
So-so. Despite applying the lotion beforehand, the nits are still there.
Not for children under 3. One month of use and my daughter still had lice and nits.
Don't like it. Not practical — you have to wait at least 15 minutes before rinsing. Plus it doesn't really do its job.
Mixed. Washes hair well but is not a preventive lice treatment.
Not convinced. After an invasion of lice on my daughter, difficult to get rid of nits so I bought this shampoo. It wasn't useful for easier nit removal as advertised.
Top. Pleasant smell and easy to massage in. My children like to wash their hair with it.
Good but expensive.
Great product for peacefully facing another school year with lice cases.
Pleasant smell to use as prevention.
Effective. I bought it in September as lice prevention. End of December — no lice in sight. Unlike last year when I spent weeks treating.
Useful and effective, without the hair smelling of anti-lice.
Good product. Pleasant fragrance and effective for repelling lice.
Eh.
Shampoo for cleaning dead lice and nits. Aggressive — cleans the scalp so much my daughter's scalp itched, but it also removed dandruff along with dead nits the metal comb couldn't remove.

Good product, bad delivery. The product is fine, it works, but delivery failed. First shipment lost. Second arrived in a kraft envelope with broken cap. Options were resend or exchange with return only.
Good. Good price-quality ratio.
Perfect. Perfect product for regular use, doesn't damage hair and protects very well. Recommend.
Works. Worked well.
Good to use. Easy to use, smells good, no itchy scalp.

100% repeat-buy product. Absolutely satisfied with the shampoo. Since I regularly wash the kids' hair with it, lice are history. We apply a pharmacy protective spray afterwards — done! Smells good too.
Super.
Lice shampoo. Mega shampoo. I experimented a lot, nothing really helped. Then found this product and it helped.
Wonderful. You notice the difference even from the first application, and the scent is pleasant — a great product.
Very effective. I was skeptical when I ordered, but it really helps.
Fulfills its purpose. Super effect, easy application, will certainly use again for a new outbreak. Cheaper than at the pharmacy.
Usable. Helped perfectly and applicable.

Lice are gone. Great product, super functional. We tried all kinds of lice products. The most important thing with all of them is combing the hair! Most parents forget this — and combing can be painful on long hair.
Not effective. Despite treatment with the shampoo the night before and spray in the morning, my daughter got lice from school. Pleasant smell and feel, but unfortunately not effective.
Product leaked.
Shampoo doesn't work. My daughter had lice and did a base treatment with a pharmacy product — she was lice-free. Then she used this shampoo preventively. But she still got another infestation.
Not suitable. Itchy scalp was the result. Maybe OK if you have very oily hair. The smell is so-so. Not a pleasant hair wash.
Pointless. I ordered it thinking it helps with lice — unfortunately this kids' lice shampoo does nothing, my child keeps bringing the critters home.
Dries hair out. Doesn't foam and spreads poorly.
Unfortunately without success. No noticeable effect.
Doesn't do anything. Smells nice but doesn't help.
Unfortunately didn't work that way. Sticky and hair is greasy afterwards.
Disappointed. The bottle isn't full, liquid is missing.
Doesn't work for everyone. Didn't work for us.
Packaging is very bad. The product isn't sealed, about a quarter of the contents spilled into the bag.
Not super. It seems it helps a little.
Smells good, delivery issue. Nicely wrapped in plastic bag, but a lot leaked out. Shame at this price. Smell pleasant, product looks good.

Effectiveness. We had lice recently and I don't know if this shampoo would have helped. My head still itches even though nothing's there. Maybe it dries the scalp. Whether it helps preventively, I don't know.
Lice shampoo. Was disappointed, unfortunately no effect.

A bit expensive, but since then no incidents. We never had problems with lice until two years ago — kept coming back from school and Kita. Spent ages with lice shampoos. Since using this one weekly, lice and the comb are permanently past. Kids like it, so the price is OK.

Nourishing and pleasant scent. Whether it actually protects against head lice I can't judge. Mentally helped my daughter return to school because she was afraid of getting lice again. Also nourishes hair and smells pleasant.
Works for me. Used for years. Not miraculous but she hasn't caught lice since we use this plus repellent spray.
Recommended. Good smell, good foam when washing, my daughter has done very well with it. A bit expensive but worth it.
Fragrant! Satisfied.

Finally lice-free. We've had this shampoo for 3 months now and since then we're finally lice-free. Before we couldn't get rid of them. Use it 1x a week — thrilled. The bottle is still half full. When empty, will definitely buy again.
Top article. Foams very well, pleasant smell, my children like it too.
A great smelling shampoo. Given the positive reviews, I tried it — treat the kids and myself preventively once a week. Good pleasant smell.
Satisfied. I hope 'bye lice' — easy to apply and lather. My daughter has probably picked up lice from the bus so I hope to avoid it. Scent is fruity.
Top. Very good.
Great product. Survived the entire lice season at school completely lice-free! And the hair shines beautifully.
Nice. Very good.
Would buy it again and again. Helps very well.
Usable and helpful. Helped us get through the intensive lice season at Kita well.
Shampoo. Super.
Helps prevent lice. Been using this shampoo longer and reordered. My daughter uses it 1x a week preventively and we haven't had lice so far. Smell is OK and not too strong.
Doesn't help with lice infestation. Didn't have the desired effect, lice are still alive.
Damaged. The product arrived damaged and open.
Doesn't work. Functionality nil. Lice are still crawling in the hair even after 2 hours.
Useless. Bought it hoping we wouldn't have lice. Applied as described and not 2 days later my daughter came home with lice. Absolutely disappointed, money out the window.

No effect. I used it exactly as instructed on my child since lice were going around at school. The spray didn't protect her — she came back from school with lice anyway. Now I'm fighting a battle against them. Three different products used, nothing works. These products do nothing.
Lice spray useless. Lice alarm at Kita and unfortunately my child got them. I bought this spray thinking it would help — it doesn't. Next day my child brought them home again.
Shoddy. Product arrives completely crushed and slightly open. Not satisfied — next time I'll go to the local pharmacy.
Well... I could have put water on my children's hair. Zero help.
Bottle defective. The spray nozzle doesn't work! I can't even judge it. Have to decant it somewhere.
Delivered defective, impossible to use. Spray nozzle was broken inside. Not a single drop came out.
Lice despite regular use. My daughter got lice even though I used it every day.
Defective spray bottle.

Short duration, bad price-performance ratio. Pretty disappointed. Lice are constantly at Kita and I'm tired of washing all stuffed animals weekly. Thought this spray would be a great solution. But: no leaflet, packaging says 4 applications, apply 2x per week — so it lasts just 2 weeks theoretically. With two kids it's one week. Doesn't fit for me.

Stringy hair. Probably helps lice not 'hold on', but so oily that hair looks stringy and unwashed. Nice smell but unnecessary. Doesn't help against lice — could be scent-free. Price for 4 applications is way too high. Sticking with anti-lice gummies and regular checks.
Unfortunately not effective.
Stream too thin. The sprayer isn't good — only a thin stream comes out, not a good atomizer.

Good effect but not very productive on long hair. The spray really seems to work — so far spared from lice. Regularly applied when Kita reports cases. Scent pleasant, easy application. But the bottle runs out very fast with long hair. Need a lot of product.

We disposed of it. Applied it daily to my daughter's hair for 3 weeks. Smells pleasant, hair felt great. She didn't get lice — but the Kita outbreak was mild. Her healthy long hair became very brittle. Can't say for sure it was the spray, but won't use it anymore.

So far it does what it should. Ordered because lice are around at school. She hasn't caught any, but not sure if it's thanks to the product. Hair looks a bit greasy/sticky after spraying. Smell is intense but not bad.
Pretty good. Sticks hair together a bit.
Well... I can't confirm the effectiveness. But probably varies head to head. Smell is quite intense and too strong for my kids.

Lice despite protection. Read positive reviews and decided to try it during lice alert. Applied to both children's hair — one got lice, the other didn't. Don't know if it was the hair structure but it didn't help both. Previously used a more effective but strong-smelling product. This one has neutral scent and kids tolerated it, but it didn't protect them. At least better than nothing.
Hmm, not quite. Don't find it quite right.
Not very effective. It's not that effective — better options in this price range.
It's OK.
Super. Good.
I explain how it works and its drawback.

Easy to use, smells nice. Pleasant scent and simple to apply, no mess. Used a few times on my kid's hair — didn't feel sticky or heavy. Can't say for sure if it always works but happy with how gentle it is. Worth trying for peace of mind.

Maybe we just got lucky. Bought when the lice epidemic at my son's school wouldn't end. We were spared, don't know if it's the spray. A bit oily but not too bad — you can still go out without looking unwashed.
Very pleasant. Pleasant to use — like a conditioner. Leaves shiny, easy-to-comb hair. Only issue was the sprayer mechanism — thin and fragile. Broke on first bottle, exchanged it.
Good.
Good product and quick delivery!
Doesn't stink. Compared to other sprays it has a pleasant smell. Spray bottle works well.

Well-tolerated spray. Lice going around at Kita, two kids already infested. We immediately applied preventively. Result: we were spared from lice, no nits. Spray seems to work. One point off for chemical smell. Well tolerated on short hair and scalp.
Lice spray. Bought because of a lice case at Kita. Easy to use, smells a bit chemical. Don't know if safe for years of prevention. Curious if it'll help.
Top. Works fast and as promised.

It really helps. Spray was recommended by friends to prevent lice at Kita. It actually helped. I was panicked my daughter with long hair would bring lice from Kita but we used it diligently and it helped. Smells very pleasant and sprays well. Would always buy preventively.
Helps well — lice-free for over 1 year.

Clear recommendation. We regularly use Hedrin Protect & Go spray, especially when there's a Kita lice notice — very satisfied. Since consistent use, no lice — gives a great feeling of security. Easy and fast in daily life. Spreads well without greasing or smelling unpleasant. Great with kids — used without discussion. Doesn't replace regular checks but as a preventive measure very helpful.
Satisfied. Easy to spray and smells fruity.
Highly recommended. Since using the defense spray on our kids we've had no problems with head lice anymore, even though there have been repeated cases at daycare and school. Highly recommend.
Does its job. Every few weeks there are lice at Kita. I spray preventively on my daughter's head and tie her hair up. So far we've been spared.
Super. Feel like it works — no more lice brought home from school since then.
Product. Practical.
Good. Bought it as prophylaxis.
